name: The Gorafe desert by gravel bike
name_original: El desierto de Gorafe en bici de gravel
description:
You are about to move to another planet. It does not matter what you have been told or what you have read about the Gorafe desert: it will surprise you, it will hook you.

There are almost 70 kilometers and just over 1,100 meters of positive elevation gain that are hard, although, yes, they will fly by thanks to the amazing scenery.

When you start pedaling from the town of Gorafe and immediately take the short but intense cement climb, you begin to get an idea of where you are going and that the day is going to be special. Little by little you gain height and take perspective on the valley and on the scale of the surrounding geomorphological formations. A couple more corners and suddenly it's over: you're on what looks like a plateau above the valley. Below you see the town, the river and the stone walls. Behind you, you see a huge, seemingly flat expanse. And you start pedaling along the track that goes north.

You leave behind the fields of almond trees and a couple of cave houses hanging on the wall. You have reached the Puntal de Don Diego viewpoint. Support the bike and approach the path to the esplanade of the geodesic vertex. Now you can see where you are going: you are entering the Gorafe desert.

The next 25 kilometres, until leaving the town of Villanueva de las Torres, make up the first part of the circular desert route. Wide tracks with good pavement, incredible ravines and an alien landscape.

After a short stretch of road, you will arrive at the town of Dehesas de Guadix, the last opportunity to replenish liquids and food until the end of the Route. Here begins the second part; The route goes back into the desert, this time along paths that are sometimes narrower, with good tracks and some sections along boulevards.

Reserve energy: the last 14 kilometers of the Route are uphill, most of the time flat but with a couple of notable slopes.

You just have to go down the cemented ramp to the town of Gorafe, recharge your batteries in one of its bars and enjoy having completed a stage that you will surely save among your favorites.

Some advices:

- You can visit the magnificent museum of the Megalithic Interpretation Center in Gorafe to round off the day.
- The wider the tires you have on your bike, the more comfortable the Route will be.
- To visit the Gorafe desert, the ideal is that it is not hot or that it has recently rained; so early winter or spring can be good dates.
- As in any good worthy desert, temperature variations during the day and at night are high. Bring a spare coat in case it gets late.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Gorafe, located within the Granada Geopark, offers routes through a distinctive desert environment. The region is characterized by extensive badlands, sculpted into a labyrinth of ravines, canyons, and unique "fairy chimneys." A high stone plain dramatically collapses into numerous gorges, providing varied terrain for gravel cyclists. This semi-desert landscape, including areas like "Los Coloraos" with its reddish geological formations, presents a challenging and immersive experience. Best gravel bike trails around Gorafe The most popular gravel bike trail is The Gorafe desert by gravel bike, a 41.5 miles (66.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route traverses the unique desert landscape of Gorafe. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Gorafe, a difficult 54.8 miles (88.1 km) path. This loop explores the dramatic canyons and desert terrain surrounding Gorafe. Local gravel bikers also love the Gorafe Desert – Los Coloraos Desert loop from Gorafe, a 66.2 miles (106.6 km) trail leading through the striking Los Coloraos Desert, often completed in about 8 hours 42 minutes. Gravel biking around Gorafe is defined by its badlands, deep canyons, and extensive network of tracks. The region offers routes ranging from easier paths to challenging rides requiring advanced skills. The routes in Gorafe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 240 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Gorafe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe offers around two dozen gravel bike trails. While most are categorized as difficult, there are a few moderate options available for those seeking a less intense ride through the unique desert landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Gorafe?
A: The terrain in Gorafe is incredibly diverse and often described as otherworldly. You'll encounter extensive badlands, deep ravines, dramatic canyons, and unique 'fairy chimneys.' Routes can range from wide tracks with good pavement to narrower paths, all set against a backdrop of a high stone plain that collapses into countless gorges. Areas like 'Los Coloraos' feature striking reddish geological formations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or beginner gravel bike routes in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe's gravel biking routes are predominantly challenging, with most categorized as difficult. There are a few moderate options, but no easy trails are listed. Riders should be prepared for varied terrain and significant elevation changes, which may require some advanced riding skills or even bike pushing on certain sections. For families or beginners, it's advisable to consult local guides or rental services for tailored advice on the most suitable paths.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can explore while gravel biking in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can cycle through the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt; with their distinctive reddish hues. For breathtaking panoramas, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts a significant Megalithic Park with ancient dolmens, offering a cultural dimension to your ride. Many routes traverse the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2793795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Canyons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in Gorafe?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike trails in Gorafe are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt; explores the dramatic canyons, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Los Coloraos Desert loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the striking red desert formations.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe's semi-desert climate means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for gravel biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are cooler but often sunny, though some routes might be affected by occasional rain, which can make certain sections muddy or more challenging.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the gravel bike trails in Gorafe?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the expansive and relatively quiet nature of the Gorafe desert generally makes it suitable for cycling with well-behaved dogs, especially on wider tracks. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash where required, and be mindful of the challenging terrain, potential wildlife, and the lack of shade and water sources, especially during warmer months. It's always best to check local signage or with local authorities for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the gravel bike trails in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe is a small village, and while it offers basic amenities, the trails themselves traverse a largely wild and undeveloped desert landscape. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks. For accommodation, you might find options in Gorafe itself or nearby towns. The wider region is also known for its historic cave dwellings, some of which are available for unique stays.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about gravel biking in Gorafe?
A: The komoot community highly rates gravel biking in Gorafe, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's unique and 'alien-like' desert landscapes, the sense of adventure, and the extensive network of tracks that allow for immersive exploration of its geological wonders and ancient history. The dramatic badlands, canyons, and stunning rock formations are frequently highlighted as major draws.

Q: Are there any natural thermal pools or places for relaxation near the gravel biking routes?
A: Yes, after a day of exploring the challenging gravel trails, you can find relaxation at nearby natural thermal pools. The hot springs at Balneario Alicún de las Torres are a notable option in the wider region, offering a soothing respite.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of gravel bike trails in Gorafe?
A: Gravel bike trails in Gorafe vary significantly in length and duration. For instance, a popular route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e824991060/the-gorafe-desert-by-gravel-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gorafe desert by gravel bike&lt;/a&gt; is about 41.5 miles (66.7 km) and typically takes around 7 hours to complete. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint loop from Central Hidroeléctrica de Negratín&lt;/a&gt;, can extend over 68 miles (109 km) and take more than 10 hours, depending on your pace and stops.

Q: Is public transport available to access the gravel biking areas in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe is a more remote destination within the Granada Geopark. While there might be limited local bus services to Gorafe village, direct public transport access to specific trailheads within the extensive desert landscape is generally not available. Most visitors rely on personal vehicles or dedicated shuttle services to reach starting points for their gravel biking adventures.
tips:
The Gorafe Desert is located in La Hoya de Guadix, a natural plain plenty of gullies and badlands in the northern part of the province of Granada.

La Hoya de Guadix, with a landscape deeply marked by the erosion of the basins of the Rivers Fardes and Guadix, is one of the oldest human settlements in the Iberian Peninsula, containing the largest concentration of cave dwellings and dolmens in Europe.

The picturesque village of Gorafe stands at the bottom of the Gor Canyon and it is considered the entrance to the desert.

Los Coloraos is one of the most popular areas of the desert, owing its name to the predominant red colour and its similarity to the Grand Canyon.

Although the sunset and the sunrise are probably the best moments to enjoy the amazing landscape and the dynamic shapes of the Gorafe Desert, the area also offers a spectacular night sky.
From here the Desierto Gorafe lies at your feet. Great panoramas at any time of the day or year. Absolute must on gravel and mountain bike tours.

The large gullies and colourful sandstone formations make the Gorafe Desert one of the most amazing landscapes in Europe.

Los Coloraos is one of its most popular areas, owing its name to the predominant red colour and its similarity to the Grand Canyon.

The picturesque village of Gorafe stands at the bottom of the Gor Canyon and it is considered the entrance to the desert.

Although the sunset and the sunrise are probably the best moments to enjoy the amazing landscape and the dynamic shapes of the Gorafe Desert, the area also offers a spectacular night sky.
If it's raining or has recently rained, don't even think about going down the ravines: it's impossible to pedal on such sticky clay.
